title: rubus × mirabilis
intro: Rubus × mirabilis, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the rose family and is a cross between the American thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the Western thimbleberry (Rubus lasiococcus). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus × mirabilis include its ability to aid in digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and potentially lower blood sugar levels. Rubus × mirabilis can be consumed as a dietary supplement or herbal preparation, and its leaves, stems, and roots can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
